steps.title: Keep the object with the verb
steps.content: The phrase after the blank, “repetitive improvisation exercises,” is the direct object of “include.” A transitive verb should flow directly into its object without punctuation between them.

steps.title: Remove the extra mark
steps.content: Choice A uses a colon, Choice C uses a semicolon, and Choice D uses a comma. Each mark wrongly separates “include” from its direct object. The sentence needs the unpunctuated verb phrase “may include repetitive improvisation exercises.” The answer is B.
